content: Anorexia: nutritional rehabilitation is priority (medical monitoring for refeeding syndrome), Family-Based Treatment (FBT/Maudsley) first-line for adolescents, CBT-E (enhanced cognitive behavioral therapy) or MANTRA for adults. Weight restoration precedes psychological work. Bulimia/BED: CBT-E first-line, IPT alternative. SSRIs (fluoxetine high dose for bulimia) reduce binge/purge frequency. Lisdexamfetamine FDA-approved for moderate-severe BED. Medical monitoring essential (electrolytes, cardiac status, bone density). Higher levels of care (residential, inpatient) for severe cases. Multidisciplinary team approach (medical, nutritional, psychological) optimal. Relapse prevention critical.
